jokes.text: Yeah, you're asking us to predict the future, Dee. How can I predict the future?
jokes.setup: Dee asks what they're doing tonight
jokes.punchline: Treating a simple social question as an impossible prophecy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Mac's inability to grasp simple social concepts delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Dee, you tried this shit 30 years ago when that show first came on the air.
jokes.setup: Dee wants to go to a martini bar like Sex and the City
jokes.punchline: Charlie claiming Sex and the City aired 30 years ago
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete disconnect from pop culture timeline is character-consistent

jokes.text: I did not poop the bed! / Did you say something about pooping in a bed?
jokes.setup: Charlie denies pooping the bed mid-conversation
jokes.punchline: Mac's delayed processing and horror at the revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and structure - the casual denial followed by shocked realization

jokes.text: Well, yesterday we wake up and there's a piece of poop right in the middle of me and Frank, okay?
jokes.setup: The guys want details about the poop incident
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact description of finding poop between two grown men
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual delivery of deeply disturbing information is peak character comedy

jokes.text: So we threw the first piece of poop out 'cause big deal, whatever. Accidents happen, right?
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened to the first poop
jokes.punchline: Treating bed-pooping as a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: The casual 'accidents happen' about bed-defecation is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Now, the second poop, you held onto it, right? Brought it along.
jokes.punchline: Assuming they naturally brought the evidence with them
jokes.explanation: Mac's logical approach to completely illogical situation

jokes.text: Dee, when we said we can't predict the future, how could we possibly have seen something this awesome coming?
jokes.setup: Earlier joke about predicting the future
jokes.punchline: The poop mystery is too amazing to have predicted
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reframes the earlier joke while showing Dennis's twisted priorities

jokes.text: You're not going to go out with me tonight 'cause these idiots found two poopies in a bed?
jokes.setup: Dee realizes the guys are abandoning her plans
jokes.punchline: Her infantilizing 'poopies' while being outraged
jokes.explanation: Dee's use of 'poopies' while being genuinely upset captures her character perfectly

jokes.text: But it's not the poop. It's the mystery behind the poop.
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating this like a philosophical investigation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dennis's pretentious nature applied to the most base subject matter

jokes.text: Why did you jump out from behind a car? Were you stalking me?
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption of stalking behavior
jokes.explanation: Sets up Dee's creepy behavior that will be revealed

jokes.text: That's the way girls are. We're catty. We backstab and talk about each other behind our backs and stalk.
jokes.setup: Dee claiming girls hate each other
jokes.punchline: Casually adding 'and stalk' to typical female stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Dee's complete misunderstanding of normal female relationships

jokes.text: I read all about it in the notebook Charlie keeps of your whereabouts.
jokes.setup: Dee knows the Waitress will be alone
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing Charlie's stalking behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfectly reveals both Charlie's creepiness and Dee's casual acceptance of it

jokes.text: Frederick is your cat and he died three weeks ago.
jokes.punchline: Dee's brutal takedown including dead pet
jokes.explanation: Dee's casual cruelty delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: We want you to examine our poopie.
jokes.punchline: The childish 'poopie' in a formal request
jokes.explanation: The contrast between formal scientific request and childish language

jokes.text: You have a hole in the back of your pajamas. / It's not my poop! / You've been sleeping in a giant dress shirt.
jokes.punchline: Visual of Charlie's makeshift sleepwear with hole
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Charlie's pathetic sleeping attire combined with Frank's accusation

jokes.text: I feel like you're going to say something else right there and then you stopped, right? / Please get out of my lab.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's oblivious persistence despite clear rejection
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete inability to read social cues

jokes.text: You just missed out on an extraordinary adventure, pal.
jokes.punchline: Charlie frames poop examination as a missed opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie optimism, reframing rejection as the scientist's loss. Shows his delusional worldview.

jokes.text: You got turd? / Yeah, we got a turd. / I'll take a look at it.
jokes.punchline: Immediate enthusiasm for examining feces
jokes.explanation: Simple setup-punchline but effective escalation

jokes.text: Whoever it was seems to have been eating newspaper.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of eating newspaper
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd detail that fits Charlie's character without naming him

jokes.text: Which one of you idiots was eating a goddamn newspaper? / It's gonna go both ways, dude.
jokes.setup: Mac demands to know who ate newspaper
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual admission that they both do it
jokes.explanation: Charlie's matter-of-fact delivery about eating newspaper is perfectly in character

jokes.text: This appears to be a piece of a credit card. / Inconclusive. / How is that not specific to one of you?
jokes.punchline: Charlie treating eating credit cards as normal for both
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity of what they both apparently consume

jokes.text: This is wolf hair. / Wolf air. / Also inconclusive.
jokes.punchline: Charlie mishearing and still calling it inconclusive
jokes.explanation: Charlie's mishearing adds layer to already absurd situation

jokes.text: There is so much wolf hair in our apartment right now, man. / But why are you eating it? / Well, you ingest everything. / Both of you are eating wolf?
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual 'you ingest everything' philosophy
jokes.explanation: Charlie's worldview that 'you ingest everything' is perfectly absurd logic

jokes.text: No, I'm an Econ major. I just wanted to look at some poop.
jokes.punchline: He's just a student who wanted to see poop
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - reveals their 'expert' is just a curious college kid

jokes.text: I'm gonna take off my bra, blast my nips.
jokes.setup: Guys are playing hard to get
jokes.punchline: Artemis's immediate nuclear option
jokes.explanation: Artemis's complete lack of subtlety and 'blast my nips' phrasing

jokes.text: Burmington Coat Factory. I got it on sale. It's irregular.
jokes.punchline: Pride in discount irregular clothing
jokes.explanation: Artemis's complete lack of shame about discount shopping

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I have a drinking problem.
jokes.setup: Dee says water is unattractive to announce
jokes.punchline: Waitress's matter-of-fact admission
jokes.explanation: Waitress's casual honesty about serious problem

jokes.text: Nothing with 'bombs' in the name, okay?
jokes.setup: Artemis ordering increasingly inappropriate bomb drinks
jokes.punchline: Bartender's exhausted blanket policy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and practical bartender response

jokes.text: Name's Artemis. I have a bleached asshole.
jokes.punchline: Immediate oversharing as pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis character moment - shocking overshare delivered with complete confidence. Iconic line.

jokes.text: My legs get hot.
jokes.punchline: Frank's practical explanation for sleeping nearly naked
jokes.explanation: Frank's matter-of-fact reasoning for inappropriate attire

jokes.text: Oh, maybe I'll get two TVs and two refrigerators. / Do we come to your house and tell you how to sleep?
jokes.punchline: Charlie's sarcastic escalation and defensive response
jokes.explanation: Charlie's defensive anger about his living situation

jokes.text: Dennis on his bed made for kings with his toilet made out of gold.
jokes.setup: Dennis bragging about normal sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Charlie's bitter sarcastic embellishment
jokes.explanation: Charlie's class resentment turning normal amenities into luxury

jokes.text: Charlie, we sleep ass to ass, you know that.
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual correction about their intimate arrangement
jokes.explanation: Frank's matter-of-fact description of deeply disturbing sleeping arrangement

jokes.text: I sleep in a king-sized bed by myself. When I have to go to the bathroom, I use a toilet.
jokes.setup: Frank's defensive response about their living situation
jokes.punchline: Dennis describing basic human living standards as if they're luxuries
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis superiority moment. The implication that using a toilet is noteworthy highlights how low Charlie and Frank's standards are.

jokes.text: It smells like some kind of spice blend or pesticide, or some other kind of sandy poison or something.
jokes.punchline: Escalating descriptions of horrific floor conditions
jokes.explanation: Mac's escalating horror at Charlie's living conditions

jokes.text: And you, you shouldn't be drinking in a shoe store.
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're drinking inappropriately while shopping
jokes.explanation: Reveals their classy night has devolved to drinking in shoe stores

jokes.text: 13. / We don't carry shoes that large. Perhaps you'd like to try the Big and Tall store down the street?
jokes.punchline: Revealing Dee's enormous feet and salesperson's brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Dee's physical comedy - her bird-like features include huge feet

jokes.text: Probably protecting me. I spent a lot of money today.
jokes.punchline: Her delusional rationalization
jokes.explanation: Dee's inability to accept embarrassing reality

jokes.text: Frank, I will jam that poop right back up your ass where it came from.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's graphic threat
jokes.explanation: Charlie's violent imagery delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: I could probably squeeze something out. / I'll get some newspaper.
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual ability and Charlie's helpful newspaper retrieval
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to newspaper eating while showing their casual attitude toward defecation

jokes.text: He was turtle-heading.
jokes.punchline: Dennis's clinical observation using crude terminology
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pretentious use of crude slang term

jokes.text: I'm just gonna go get more wasted, and then I'm gonna bang some rim, dude.
jokes.setup: Artemis fed up with the situation
jokes.punchline: Her crude exit strategy announcement
jokes.explanation: Artemis's completely inappropriate oversharing continues

jokes.text: This is an interesting piece. / I love his use of space. / Look at the rich texture, the dimensions. / It's almost as if you can smell it.
jokes.setup: Art critics examining what they think is art
jokes.punchline: Pretentious analysis of literal poop
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of pretentious art criticism applied to literal feces

jokes.text: It's almost as if it exploded out of the artist. / Well... I think it's crap.
jokes.punchline: Dennis's literal truth delivered as art criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect double meaning - Dennis dismissing 'art' while literally stating what it is

jokes.text: Four turds, five suspects, so many, many nameless victims.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic narration about poop mystery
jokes.explanation: Mac's film noir approach to the poop investigation

jokes.text: Tall order for such a short man.
jokes.setup: Mac theorizing about Frank's pooping capacity
jokes.punchline: Height-based poop production analysis
jokes.explanation: Mac's logical approach to illogical situation

jokes.text: And like a small brown snail... it crept to the middle between Charlie and Frank.
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of feces migration
jokes.explanation: Mac's unexpectedly poetic imagery for disgusting subject

jokes.text: And like a small brown snail... it crept to the middle between Charlie and Frank.
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of poop sliding down slanted bed
jokes.explanation: Beautiful absurdist poetry applied to poop physics. The snail metaphor is both disgusting and poetic.

jokes.text: The outcast? The slut? The bitch. The whore. The lonely, sad, slutty, bitchy whore.
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults ending in redundant combination
jokes.explanation: Mac's dramatic escalation becomes redundant and self-defeating

jokes.text: Thunder of the... chocolate variety.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic 'chocolate thunder'
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt at euphemism for defecation

jokes.text: Known the world over for his ability to replicate any man's stool, Cricket came back and committed fecal forgery.
jokes.punchline: Cricket as a professional poop impersonator
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept of Cricket as feces forger - perfect escalation

jokes.text: A turd merger... fused together by fear and hatred... and hair.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining two poops becoming one
jokes.punchline: Corporate merger terminology for poop combination
jokes.explanation: Business terminology applied to feces, plus callback to wolf hair

jokes.text: I did it. / I did 'em all. / I did everyone of them. / I even did one, while she was rappin us, right here.
jokes.punchline: Frank's simple confession and current action
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Mac's elaborate theories with Frank's simple truth

jokes.text: Because poop is funny.
jokes.setup: Asked why he did all the poops
jokes.punchline: Frank's simple, honest motivation
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on the episode itself - Frank speaks for the show's philosophy
